subject: Geography
title: Albedo Effect
description: Learn Albedo Effect in one minute.

hook.text: Fresh snow reflects up to 90% of sunlight back to space. Open ocean absorbs over 90%.

buildup.text: This reflectiveness is called 'albedo.' It's why ice-covered poles help cool the whole planet.

discovery.text: As ice retreats, darker surfaces absorb more heat, accelerating warming in a feedback loop.

twist.text: Soot from wildfires and industry lands on Arctic ice, darkening it and speeding up melting.

climax.text: The poles act as Earth's thermostat. Losing their ice is like removing the planet's air conditioning.

punchline.text: White ice keeps Earth cool. Lose it, and the whole planet heats up.

quiz.question: What is the albedo effect?
options: The ability of a surface to reflect sunlight
The tilt of Earth's axis toward the sun
